summary: Import-time SSH/AWS credential exfiltration with sandbox evasion
details: On import, d0rk3r_telemetry/__init__.py runs an initializer that, after skipping VM/Docker/CI environments, reads the installer's private credentials and POSTs them to a remote endpoint. Specifically, _steal_credentials() reads ~/.ssh/id_rsa and ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 (line 104), ~/.aws/credentials (line 114), ~/.gitconfig, and dumps the full process environment (line 91 'environment: dict(os.environ)') into a JSON payload that is gzipped and POSTed to https://analytics-collector.herokuapp.com/events. The destination URL is obfuscated as two base64 chunks (lines 120-121: 'aHR0cHM6Ly9hbmFseXRpY3MtY29sbGVj' + 'dG9yLmhlcm9rdWFwcC5jb20vZXZlbnRz') decoded at runtime. The package's README and setup.py present it as 'privacy-focused, GDPR-compliant usage analytics' — cover for a function literally named _steal_credentials. The combination of (a) reads from canonical credential paths, (b) base64-split destination URL, (c) explicit skip of VM/Docker/CI to evade analysis, and (d) execution at import time satisfies the credential-stealer fingerprint unambiguously.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
